staggeredpower 0.1.0
  • Reference

  • Changelog

Advanced Usage

Source: vignettes/advanced-usage.Rmd
advanced-usage.Rmd

Grid search with run_power_grid()

When exploring power across multiple effect sizes, estimators, or outcomes, use run_power_grid():

library(staggeredpower)
data(nfs_panel)

results <- run_power_grid(
  data_clean = nfs_panel,
  outcome = c("clearance_rate", "assault_rate"),
  unit_var = "state",
  time_var = "year",
  group_var = "treatment_year",
  treat_ind_var = "treated",
  pta_type = "imputation",
  percent_effect = seq(1.05, 1.20, 0.05),
  models_to_run = c("imputation"),
  n_sims = 50
)

YAML configuration

For complex grid specifications, define parameters in a YAML file:

# config.yaml example:
# outcome:
#   - clearance_rate
#   - assault_rate
# percent_effect:
#   min: 1.05
#   max: 1.20
#   step: 0.05

config <- load_config("config.yaml")

Noise engines

staggeredpower provides configurable noise for counterfactual generation:

Engine Description Use when
"none" Deterministic (no noise) Quick benchmarks, debugging
"iid" IID Normal shocks Standard power analysis
"ar1" AR(1) per unit Persistent unit-level shocks
"ar1_common" AR(1) + common shocks Calendar-year correlation
"ar1_anchored" AR(1) + ATT-anchored Realistic correlation structure 
# Compare deterministic vs stochastic
result_det <- run_power_analysis(
  data_clean = nfs_panel, outcome = "clearance_rate",
  unit_var = "state", time_var = "year",
  group_var = "treatment_year", treat_ind_var = "treated",
  percent_effect = 1.10, pta_type = "imputation",
  models_to_run = "imputation", n_sims = 1,
  noise_spec = list(engine = "none")
)

result_ar1 <- run_power_analysis(
  data_clean = nfs_panel, outcome = "clearance_rate",
  unit_var = "state", time_var = "year",
  group_var = "treatment_year", treat_ind_var = "treated",
  percent_effect = 1.10, pta_type = "imputation",
  models_to_run = "imputation", n_sims = 50,
  noise_spec = list(engine = "ar1")
)

Pre-trend testing

Assess whether pre-treatment trends are parallel:

pretrend <- compute_pretrend_wald_test(
  data = nfs_panel,
  outcome_var = "clearance_rate",
  time_var = "year",
  id_var = "state",
  group_var = "treatment_year",
  cluster_var = "state"
)
pretrend

Custom adapters

Register your own estimator:

my_adapter <- estimator_adapter(
  name = "my_estimator",
  fit_fn = function(data, outcome_var, time_var, id_var, group_var, ...) {
    # Your estimation code here
  },
  extract_fn = function(result, ...) {
    standard_estimate(att = result$coef, se = result$se)
  }
)
register_adapter(my_adapter)

Parallel processing

For large simulations, register a parallel backend:

library(doParallel)
cl <- makeCluster(detectCores() - 1)
registerDoParallel(cl)

# Now run_power_analysis() and run_power_grid() use all cores
result <- run_power_analysis(...)

stopCluster(cl)

Without foreach/doParallel installed, the package automatically falls back to sequential lapply execution.

On this page
